title: More Developers =/= Faster? Why Teams DON'T Scale! #shorts
summary: Cyril Imhof argues that adding more developers to engineering teams does not proportionally increase output due to communication overhead and coordination complexity. Effective scaling requires intentional team design rather than simple headcount growth.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof challenges the assumption that adding developers increases output in proportion to headcount. The argument: communication overhead and coordination complexity create diminishing returns, so larger teams do not scale linearly. The takeaway is that effective scaling depends on intentional team design rather than simply growing the number of engineers.
